headline: JPMorgan resets Nvidia stock price target after earnings
summary: Nvidia just delivered what analysts are calling one of the most consequential quarters in semiconductor history. The stock was already up nearly 20% year-to-date before the results landed. And JPMorgan's Harlan Sur still sees 25% upside from here. The reasoning behind that view is more nuanced than ...

headline: Hyperscaler Debt Flood Brings Derivatives Bonanza

headline: Jamie Dimon sends stark message on defense contractors
summary: Jamie Dimon does not mince words. He never has. The JPMorgan Chasechairman and chief executive officer has spent decades writing some of the most closely read annual letters on Wall Street. His 2025 shareholder letter is no different, but this year, he went further than most expected. Dimon ...

headline: How The First Citizens BancShares (FCNC.A) Investment Story Is Shifting After Analyst Target Resets
summary: The fair value estimate for First Citizens BancShares has been trimmed slightly from US$2,229.58 to US$2,212.92 per share, a reduction of about US$16.66. This adjustment aligns with Street research that has turned more cautious, with several firms cutting price targets and issuing downgrades as they reassess the balance among valuation, execution risks, and growth expectations. As you read on, you will see how these target moves are shaping the evolving analyst story around the stock and what...

headline: How $300,000 in JEPQ and JEPI Quietly Pays $24,000 a Year Without the MLP K-1 Headache
summary: A 65-year-old single retiree with $300,000 split evenly between two JPMorgan covered-call ETFs can pull roughly $24,000 in annual distributions without filing a single K-1. That math, and the trade-offs behind it, are the entire reason this strategy has become a default in retirement chat threads. The vehicles in question are JPMorgan Nasdaq Equity Premium ... How $300,000 in JEPQ and JEPI Quietly Pays $24,000 a Year Without the MLP K-1 Headache

headline: Jamie Dimon has bad news for JPMorgan bankers
summary: Every generation of Wall Street workers learns the same lesson the hard way. The bank you joined is rarely the bank you retire from. Roles get reshuffled, divisions get sold off, and the career path that looked rock-solid on day one almost never matches the one that pays out at year 30. For ...

headline: AI Could Kill the Brokerage Industry’s Cash Cow
summary: Shares of Charles Schwab and other firms have swooned on concerns that AI tools could erode the profit they derive from cash that clients hold in so-called sweep accounts.

headline: How The Quilter (LSE:QLT) Story Is Shifting As Analyst Views Diverge
summary: Quilter’s updated fair value price target now stands at £2.02 per share, compared with £2.00 previously, reflecting only a very modest shift in the analyst model. That small move comes against a backdrop of mixed Street research, where some analysts have raised targets into the low 200 GBp range while others have trimmed forecasts. This leaves you with a wide spread of opinions to weigh. Read on to see what is driving these differing views and how you can track the evolving narrative around...
